reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 125 cfs @ Fort Collins , 2 c fs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir Summer is in full swing on the Poudre, which means the bugs have been active and the fish have been too. If you're planning to fish the Poudre, be mindful that water temperatures have been exceeding 68°F, which can significantly increase stress and mortality in trout after they're caught and released. As for bugs, the menu is wide open on the Poudre. Caddis, PMDs, and Yellow Sallies have been the most active, while Golden Stones and Green Drakes have also been making appearances when conditions are right. Although there's been plenty of bug activity. Dry flies have been producing well, with consistent hatches during the early morning and again in the evening. As bug activity slows through the middle of the day, dry-dropper rigs have been especially effective, with Caddis and PMD patterns finding the most success. If you have any questions or are interested in booking a guided trip on the Poudre, feel free to give either shop a call. We'll be happy to help you get set up for a great day on the water. Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year. Or, click the images below for a full list of our current recommended fly patterns.
